summary: Tag each Agricola card with the mechanics/features it uses

cards.note: Always-on rule: one clay/stone room gains capacity for an extra person while adjacent to both a field and a pasture (dynamic adjacency check, capacity lost if adjacency lost).

cards.new_category: SCHED-PIECE: place game PIECES (stables) on future round spaces at play time, to be deployed when those rounds start
cards.note: On play schedules stables onto rounds +3/+6/+9; at the start of each such round an optional free build-stable sub-action fires (clarified: not on your turn, triggers nothing). E-SCHED is nearest but it schedules stables, not goods.
'Each time you use a wood accumulation space' fires before the space's action (clarification confirms: food gained by the space can't pay); optional pay-3-food to plow one field.

Fires after the build-rooms sub-action ('immediately' after adding rooms, growth needs the room); optional pay 1 wood+1 grain for one Family Growth (room required) regardless of rooms built. Wood/grain cost is ordinary resources.

cards.new_category: DECLINE-TRIGGER: a hook that fires when the player DECLINES an offered/unconditional sub-action (here Sow), rather than on performing it
cards.note: Trigger is declining a Sow sub-action — an unusual decline-keyed hook; grants an extra worker placement, even on occupied spaces (errata: never Meeting Place if occupied); repeatable per qualifying decline.
'Each time after you use Fishing' with <=2 food on it grants a whole Major-or-Minor-Improvement action (granted action -> optional); needs the space's food count snapshotted before it was emptied.
Immediate on-play instance plus fires each time a stone accumulation round card is revealed in preparation: 1 wood automatic (F-AUTO) plus an optional granted Minor Improvement action (F-TRIG).

Hooks receiving a Bake Bread sub-action and substitutes it ('instead of') with a free occupation play (play-a-card is a granted sub-action); errata caps it at once per turn; clarification lets it legalize using Grain Utilization otherwise unusable.
Pure end-game scoring rule: 2 bonus pts per pasture covering >=3 spaces — a plain size/coverage count, so E-SCORE only (no HOOK, not E-SCOREOPT, not L-GEOMFARM per ruling).

Fires in each returning-home phase; needs to know which spaces workers came from (>=3 from accumulation spaces -> ST-PLACELOG); optional buy of 1 bonus point for 1 food (bonus point via an action legitimately combines HOOK+E-SCORE; running bought-points tally -> ST-COUNTER).
Pure end-game scoring rule: 1 bonus pt per cooking improvement (ovens excluded per clarification) — plain count, computed at scoring.
Fires on taking food from a food accumulation space; the reward tier depends on the AMOUNT taken (needs the taken quantity -> ST-PROV), mandatory gain of 1 veg/grain/reed.

Fires in each harvest's field phase (timing within the phase is nominal); mandatory goods keyed on each wood accumulation space's current count (reading a named space's good-count is not board geometry).
Fires after each new field tile is gained (the plow sub-action, or any effect granting a field tile); mandatory +1 clay +1 food per tile.
Harvest field-phase hook: fires when a field-harvest removal empties a field (needs event payload: which field, which crop, was it the last) -> food gain; 2 food for grain, 1 for vegetable.
Fires on placing a person on a building-resource accumulation space; condition (nth placement this round, exact resource count 5/4/3) is readable from state at fire time, so no stored counter needed.
Fires on the Major/Minor Improvement action but only if an improvement was actually played from hand (needs the action's outcome payload) -> +3 food; clarification: must literally be that action.

Each use of a Lessons space places a vegetable directly onto qualifying fields (>=3 grain, no veg) — crops added under existing crops on fields, not loose goods; mixed-crop fields count as one type at a time.
Each use of a wood accumulation space grants an optional Sow sub-action limited to exactly 1 field (granted sub-actions are optional -> F-TRIG).
One-shot on play: wood equal to a plain state count (improvements in front of you) — trivially implementable.

Every harvest field phase: mandatory +1 wood (F-AUTO) plus an optional buy of up to 2 wood at 1 food each (F-TRIG; a food-paid conversion). Timing within the field phase unspecified; the parts may be split as separate decisions per clarification.
Immediate +1 clay on play, plus an explicit after-hook ("each time after you use") on Lessons spaces or Clay Pit -> +1 clay; stacks with on-play when played via Lessons (2 clay).
Standard "each time you use [space]" hook (default before) on Grain Utilization -> +2 clay +1 wood.
Explicit before-hook on using any round-space action card on fixed round spaces 5-7 -> +1 stone; fixed round bands are not board geometry per the rulings.
Explicit before-hook on round-space cards 1/2/3/4 -> wood/clay/reed/stone respectively; fixed round numbers, not L-GEOMBOARD.
Passive: the card is a person-holding slot (not a room) until a fixed deadline; a mandatory returning-home hook at round 9 removes the person from play permanently if no room exists for them.
Opponent-action hook: when the other player uses Reed Bank you may build one room (optional granted sub-action) with a material-dependent discount (1 clay / 2 stone).

"Immediately after" using Farm Expansion or Grain Seeds, optionally take the other space's full action with the same already-placed person (moves there; works even if occupied); errata caps the jump at once per turn (prevents chaining), hence CAP-TURN despite being a hook.

Optional, once-per-round redirect of a normal placement (3rd+ person) onto the space your 2nd person occupies — really a legality extension (use an occupied space, tracked via which space person #2 sits on); E-GRANTACT for re-using that space's action; 'mark the space' = a per-round used flag.

Fires after building one of 3 named majors 'in any way' (clarification) — keyed on the build-improvement sub-action, not the Maj/Min space; grants up to 2 optional play-occupation sub-actions with the occupation cost waived; needs which improvement was built (ST-PROV).

Optional hook before ANOTHER player's unconditional Sow sub-action: give them 1 grain (opponent transfer) to earn 1 bonus point (action-earned score, so HOOK+E-SCORE is legitimate); accumulated bonus points need a running counter.
On play +1 wood +1 clay; end-game bonus per pasture filled to max capacity — animal-to-pasture assignment is a genuine arrangement optimization (E-SCOREOPT).

cards.new_category: SCORE-ALLPLAYERS: an end-game bonus rule that awards points to EVERY player meeting a per-player threshold (opponents score from your card), without any comparison between players
cards.note: On-play wood scaled by rounds left (plain state read); scoring gives 1/3/5 to EACH player with 2+/3+/4+ animals of every type — not comparative (no 'most'), but grants opponents points, which no code captures.

cards.new_category: SCORE-ALLPLAYERS: an end-game bonus rule that awards points to EVERY player meeting a per-player threshold (opponents score from your card), without any comparison between players
cards.note: Same shape as A135 but for building resources of each type; clarification only refines the scoring computation (resources spent on craft-major bonuses don't count) — no extra mechanic.
'Each time you use' Sheep Market / Reed Bank (default T-BEFORE); optionally also take 1 good from the other accumulation space — a taken sheep must be accommodated (E-ANIMALS).

On play grants a Major Improvement ACTION restricted to building a Fireplace (granted action → optional at use, but the grant itself is on-play; E-GRANTACT); plus a mandatory +1 food each time you use the Hollow space (default T-BEFORE).

Mandatory bonus each time you use Clay Pit or Hollow: food equal to the clay currently on the OTHER accumulation space — a live board read at fire time, no stored state; reading a named space's good-count is explicitly not L-GEOMBOARD.
Fires at the START of each returning-home phase (T-BEFORE within the round-end seam); conditional on the occupancy of two NAMED spaces (a plain state read, not board geometry); grants 1 vegetable.
Fires on ANY player (both actor tags) taking ≥2 reed from Reed Bank; optional choice: take 1 grain OR buy 1 veg for 2 food (a food cost); needs the take-amount payload (ST-PROV).

On play stocks 3 reed + 4 clay ON the card (ST-STACK); each pile latches once to the FIRST player (possibly the opponent — hence E-OPPTRANSFER) to reach 3 pastures / 5 field tiles, not retroactively — two count-threshold one-shots watching all players.

cards.new_category: S-HEND: a hook firing at the end of a harvest (after the breeding phase completes) — no existing harvest seam covers end-of-harvest
cards.note: Mandatory 1 reed at the end of each harvest; nearest seam is after the breeding phase (T-AFTER + S-HBREED), but a true end-of-harvest seam is missing.
Mandatory bonus keyed on taking EXACTLY 2/3/4/5 food from a food accumulation space (6+ = nothing) → needs the amount-taken payload (ST-PROV); grants the matching building resource.
Card itself holds sheep; capacity = completed feeding phases, derivable from round number (no stored counter needed), always-on rule.
'Each time you use Traveling Players' (default before) grants an optional full Build Rooms action with a 1-reed-less cost modifier scoped to that action.
Fires on ANOTHER player's use of Traveling Players; grants an optional choice of a full Build Fences/Stables/Rooms action.

Start of returning-home: if exactly one round-1-4 space unoccupied, optionally use its effect without placing a person (errata: effect only, space not 'used'); round-space band 1-4 is fixed, not board geometry.
Returning-home hook conditional on no worker returning from a Lessons space (needs placement provenance); grants optional play-occupation with modified 1-food cost.
One-shot fires the first moment the 5-wild-boar count threshold is reached after play; immediate 3 bonus points, never again.
Opponent uses any food accumulation space: optionally give them 1 grain (transfer to opponent) for 1 bonus point (score earned through an action).
Opponent uses reed/stone/sheep/boar accumulation space: optionally pay them 1 food (food cost, transferred to opponent) for 1 matching good from supply; sheep/boar may need accommodation.
Start of each returning-home phase, mandatory 1 food if a Lessons space is unoccupied — plain state read, automatic gain.
Opponent uses Traveling Players: optional exchange of 1 grain/sheep/veg for 4/5/7 food — a per-trigger conversion at fixed rates.
Opponent uses Fishing/Reed Bank: optionally give them 1 wood (transfer) to get 2/3 food from supply — a conversion whose input goes to the opponent.
Fires on your accumulation-space use, conditional on having already used another accumulation space of the SAME good type this work phase — needs a log of which spaces you used; mandatory +1 veg.

Continuously makes the physical gap between Forest and Clay Pit a usable action space (board-position-dependent) whenever both are occupied; using it is a normal worker placement yielding 2 clay + 3 wood.
On your Resource Market use, mandatory bonus good keyed to the ORDINAL of the person being placed this round (1st-5th) — needs a per-round placement counter.

cards.new_category: PAY-TO-SPACE: a good spent by the effect is placed onto a board action space (re-accumulates) instead of returning to the general supply
cards.note: Fires each time you take wood from a wood accumulation space (no 'immediately after' wording -> default T-BEFORE); optional 1 wood -> 1 sheep exchange (sheep must be accommodated); the paid wood goes back onto the accumulation space; works even if 0 wood taken per clarification.
Immediately before each harvest (start-of-harvest seam), optionally buy 1 cattle for 4 food minus 1 per pasture (min 0) — a food-priced animal purchase with a plain state-read discount.

Fires after building; mandatory ('you also get') animal whose type is fixed by the room material; needs turn-scoped tracking that BOTH >=1 room AND >=1 stable were built on the same turn (ST-COUNTER).
'Immediately after' using a Lessons space (explicit T-AFTER; clarification: never fires immediately on its own play), optional buy of 1 sheep/boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food.
On play, a mandatory choice of 1 wood OR 1 grain (E-GOODS); plus a continuous rule raising house animal capacity to 1 pet per room instead of 1 total (E-CAPGROW, per-room).
Always-on legality extension: adds wood->stone as a valid renovation target (declinable per clarification); no goods, no firing.

Cost modifier scoped to the literal Build Fences action: 3 fences per such action cost no wood (a per-action free-fence budget); clarification excludes non-literal fencing effects (Mini Pasture etc.).

Recurring start-of-round option (not LATCH — the stone-house requirement is a standing gate on a repeating ability, checked each round): pay 1 food to take a plow-1-field sub-action; S-SOR has no triggering action so timing tagged T-BEFORE (fires at round start, before work).

Keyed to family growth: for 1 food the same-round offspring may take an action (an extra placement this round) and loses 'newborn' status (feeds 2 food at harvest); clarification makes the window deferrable to any time after growth within the round's work phase.
Pure end-game scoring rule (+1 per unfenced stable), computed at scoring — no hook, plain count.

On play an ordered 8-good pile is stacked on the card (ST-STACK); at any time buy the top good for 1 food (any number at once per clarification) — an at-will food-for-good exchange consuming the pile in order.

cards.new_category: PAY-TO-SPACE: a good spent by the effect is placed onto a board action space (re-accumulates) instead of returning to the general supply
cards.note: 'Immediately after' using a wood accumulation space (explicit T-AFTER), optional 1 wood -> 2 food; the spent wood is placed back onto the accumulation space; works even taking <1 wood per clarification.
Mandatory 3 food after either qualifying sub-action: building >=1 clay room, or a genuine clay->stone renovation.
After each build-room sub-action (>=1 room), optionally schedule 1 food onto each of the next 4 round spaces, collected at those rounds' starts.
On-play +1 grain; optional harvest-field-phase yield boost (+1 grain per grain field) changes the field-harvest yield rule (E-CROPMANIP); fires during/with the field phase harvest.
Each use of Day Laborer: mandatory +1 grain (F-AUTO rounds 1-5); from round 6 the mandatory gain carries a grain-or-vegetable choice (F-MANDCHOICE).
Each use of any wood accumulation space grants +1 wood automatically; standard before-the-space-action bonus.
Triggered by using one of 4 named spaces, but the wood arrives at the end of that turn (T-AFTER, end-of-turn delivery); per clarification, only on turns using those spaces.
One-shot latch on the standing condition 'no longer live in a wooden house'; places 2 clay on each of the next 5 round spaces, collected at round starts.
Optional cost substitution (2 clay OR 2 stone -> 1 wood) applied when paying for build-room/renovate; once per room built (per clarification) / once per renovate action.
One-time on-play goods gain conditional on a plain state read (clay house with exactly 2 rooms); no effect otherwise — trivially implementable.
Pure end-game bonus-point ladder over improvement count; a computed scoring rule, no hook.
Each Fishing use: optional pay 1 wood (ordinary resource cost) for food-per-person + 1 reed.
Always-on cost reduction: every improvement, room, and renovation costs 1 stone less.
Each use of the three animal-market accumulation spaces: optional buy of 1 extra matching animal for 1 food (food cost may need conversion; animal must be accommodated).
Each own use of Traveling Players grants +1 wood +1 grain automatically.
Fires on ANOTHER player's Traveling Players use: automatic 1 food + 1 wood, then an 'immediately after' optional buy of 1 vegetable for 2 food (food cost).
On-play +1 wild boar; additionally boar breed at end of round 12 (no harvest there — breeding outside the harvest breeding phase, automatic if room for the newborn).
'Each time you use Day Laborer' = before-space hook; optional grant of a build-1-room OR renovate sub-action, normal cost paid.

On-play +1 wood; then a recurring start-of-round optional build-1-stable-for-1-wood grant gated on the standing stone-house condition (recurring, so not LATCH). S-SOR is a phase seam; T-BEFORE used as the default timing tag.
Before-space hook on Day Laborer granting an optional plow-1-field sub-action.
Always-on cost modifier: major-improvement stone cost reduced by rooms added beyond the initial house (state-computed, no stored state).
Stone-house-gated recurring start-of-round optional grant of a play-occupation (at fixed 1-food cost, a card-specific food cost + cost modification) or play-minor (its normal cost) sub-action.
End-game bonus per pasture with >=1 animal and >=3 spare capacity — maximizing qualifying pastures requires optimizing the animal-to-pasture assignment.
Scoring bonus per own occupation played after this one — needs a running count of subsequent own occupation plays (or an order record); scoring itself is a passive rule.
One-shot player-count-dependent gain: 2p = 3 clay (plain goods); 4p+ variants give sheep, so E-ANIMALS applies to the card generally.
'At any time' exchange of 1 farmyard sheep for boar/veg/stone; the boar option provides an animal needing accommodation.
One-shot latch on the standing 'live in a stone house' condition: places 3 food on each remaining round space (scheduled goods), never again.
'Each time you use a wood accumulation space' = before-space hook (default ruling) granting an optional additional Bake Bread sub-action.
Explicit 'immediately before playing each occupation' hook; optional pay-1-wood (plain resource cost) for 1 food per occupation in front of you.
Start-of-round conditional gain (state-read condition: >=3 rooms, exactly 2 people): 1 food + a mandatory grain-or-vegetable choice.
Start-of-round automatic +1 wood while the standing exactly-2-rooms condition holds (recurring conditional gain, not a latch).
Bonus clay each time named accumulation spaces are used; 'each time you use' fires before, mandatory gain. 3p Clay Pit clause is inert in 2p.
One-time optional on-play purchase: pay 1 food (total) for 1 stone per room — a food-cost effect with a plain state read (room count).
Always-on replacement cost formula for new rooms (3 building resource + 2 reed).
On-play wood scaled by rounds left (fixed state read), plus a comparative end-game bonus (most rooms, both players can tie-share).
Mandatory +1 vegetable each time Grain Seeds is used; before-the-action timing per 'each time you use'.
Payment substitution (1 wood replaces the 1–2 reed) on renovate/build-room — a cost-modifier conversion applied whenever paying, not a fired event.
Each Resource Market use grants a mandatory choice of 1 clay OR 1 grain (no decline).
One-shot fires the first moment a standing condition (sole player with a 2-room house — reads both players' houses) becomes true; 'only once'.
On play, places 1 sheep on four future round spaces (current+2/5/8/10); sheep collected at round start must be accommodated.
Optional buy of 1 cattle for 1 food each time Grain Seeds is used; food cost payable via conversion, cattle must be accommodated.

Extends Build Stables legality to the center of a 2x2 of fields (adjacency/shape); that stable holds a person (new person slot) and explicitly holds no animal; a per-card once-only flag limits it to one such stable.
The card itself is an animal holder for wild boar; capacity = completed feeding phases, derivable from the round number (fixed harvest schedule), so no stored counter strictly needed.
On play: mandatory free renovation (clarification forbids playing if renovation impossible — a play prerequisite), then an optional full Build Fences action immediately after.
On using Farmland (conditional on Grain Seeds being occupied — a plain named-space occupancy read, not board geometry), optionally plow 1 extra field.

Fires each time you use Sheep Market (T-BEFORE default for 'each time you use'); grants an optional Family Growth with Room Only sub-action (F-TRIG). 'From Round 5 on' is a plain state-computable condition.

On play: choose 2/3/4 rounds and place your OWN food on those round spaces (food cost, returned later = E-SCHED + E-FOODCOST). At start of those rounds (S-SOR, timing at round start) you get the food back (E-GOODS) plus a choice of a sow sub-action (E-GRANTSUB) or a Build Fences action (E-GRANTACT); granted actions optional -> F-TRIG.
Two hooks on the Fencing space: 2 wood before the action (T-BEFORE, F-AUTO, E-GOODS), and immediately after (T-AFTER) an optional extra worker placement (F-TRIG, E-EXTRAPLACE).

On play schedules 1 wood on the next 2 odd round spaces (E-SCHED); at start of those rounds you collect the wood (E-GOODS) and get an optional Minor Improvement action (E-GRANTACT, F-TRIG). Odd round numbers are fixed, not board geometry.

Pure end-game scoring rule (no hook per ruling): 1 bonus point per own card played AFTER this one whose text contains 'accumulation space(s)' — needs a running record of qualifying cards played after it (ST-COUNTER, play-order dependent).

'Each harvest ... you can buy exactly 1' = optional deferrable buy once per harvest (ATWILL + CAP-HARVEST); costs 2 food (E-FOODCOST) for 1 bonus point earned through an action (E-SCORE via purchase, tracked over the game = ST-COUNTER). Condition (any player owns Joinery/upgrade) is a plain state read.

cards.new_category: DECLINE-TRIGGER: fires when the player deliberately declines/forgoes an action they placed for (placing on the space just to decline is legal per clarification)
cards.note: On play +1 wood +1 reed. Then each time you DECLINE a Minor/Major Improvement action you get 1 food or 1 vegetable (a choice, no decline of the gain itself -> F-MANDCHOICE); the decline seam has no existing code — nearest is S-MAJMIN.
One-shot on-play gain of goods, each conditional on a plain state read (already have >=2 of that good). Trivially implementable.

Fires immediately before each round starts (explicit T-BEFORE on S-SOR), mandatory 1 food if any goods are promised to you on remaining round spaces — a read of the scheduled-goods (E-SCHED) state, not itself a scheduling effect.

End of each preparation phase (mapped to the start-of-round seam, T-AFTER = at the end of prep): if >=1 stone in supply, +1 food (vegetable in round 14 — a fixed round number, not geometry). Mandatory -> F-AUTO.

Fires on the build-room sub-action when the built room is clay: +2 food and +1 bonus point per room (bonus points earned through an action -> HOOK + E-SCORE is legitimate; accumulated total needs ST-COUNTER). Excludes stone rooms and renovated wood rooms.

'Each time you use [that space]' -> T-BEFORE; +1 grain mandatory. The target space is the one whose card was REVEALED at the most recent pre-harvest round (4/7/9/11/13) — the round numbers are fixed but WHICH card sits there varies with the per-game reveal order, so it depends on the board's reveal arrangement (L-GEOMBOARD), and the target changes after each harvest.
On play, an optional buy: 1 grain for 1 food OR 1 vegetable for 3 food (food-paid effect -> E-FOODCOST, goods gained -> E-GOODS). The card itself is a field you can sow/harvest on -> L-CARDFIELD.

Always-on +1 capacity in each stable-less pasture (PASSIVE + E-CAPGROW). Separately, each sow sub-action (S-SUB) you may add 1 extra crop of the sown type onto that field's stack (F-TRIG) — changes what lands on the field during sowing, i.e. field-crop manipulation (E-CROPMANIP), not loose goods.
1 wood on play, plus a hook on the preparation-phase refill event: fires when reed is placed on an EMPTY Reed Bank space (a board-state condition on the refill, nature's action, no choice).
1 wood on play; then after each work phase, a mandatory conditional gain (stone>clay state read) of 1 wood.
Immediate 1 wood plus wood scheduled on the next N round spaces where N = fences already built (plain state read at play time).
Fires before using the space physically LEFT of the most recently revealed round card — depends on board arrangement/reveal order (clarified: only round cards 1-6 / 8-12 qualify).
"Each time you use" a clay/stone accumulation space -> before-hook, mandatory 1 of the opposite good.

After a fence-building sub-action that encloses >=1 genuinely NEW farmyard space (subdivision excluded — needs the new-enclosure vs subdivision distinction), get 2 stone; the per-work-phase framing is inherent to one placement per work phase.
Pure scheduling: wood/clay/reed/stone on the next 4 round spaces in fixed order.

One-time optional on-play: pay 1 stone/1 grain/1 veg/1 sheep for a Family Growth EVEN WITHOUT ROOM (no person placed — it's a granted sub-action). Round>=5 is a play condition; the cost is ordinary resources+animal.
After using the Major Improvement action space, an optional granted renovation with a chosen reduction of 2 clay OR 2 stone off its cost.
Always-on rule: the round-13 space is usable despite neighbors' workers (round space 13 is a fixed position, not board geometry).

After using Grain Utilization or Fencing while the other is unoccupied, optionally pay 1 food to use the other space with the SAME already-placed person (worker moves there, per clarification); errata caps the jump at once per turn; legality reads the other space's occupancy.

After each use of a wood accumulation space, an optional granted play-a-minor-improvement (a card-play sub-action; clarified NOT a 'Minor Improvement' action, so it must not fire Major/Minor-action hooks).

One-way latch on the standing condition 'no unused farmyard spaces' (stays on even if spaces later free up, per clarification); once latched, each vegetable harvested in the field phase accrues 1 bonus point — a running counter earned through play, not a scoring-time rule.
A one-time goods gain fired just before scoring: vegetables equal to min(majors, minors, occupations) — actual goods that then score normally, so it's a before-scoring event, not an E-SCORE rule.

Fires on your renovate-to-stone sub-action (structurally at most once/game); reward magnitude (1-3 food + 1-3 bonus pts) scales with round number — a plain state read. Bonus points earned through an action, so HOOK+E-SCORE is legitimate.
Optional start-of-round exchange (1 any-type animal + 1 grain + 1 veg -> 5 food + 2 bonus pts); cost paid in ordinary animals/crops so no cost code; points via an action so E-SCORE with the hook.

Seeds 4 goods onto the card (ST-STACK, consumed over time); fires each use of a round-space card on rounds 8-11 (fixed band, NOT board geometry); boar/cattle from the card must be accommodated (E-ANIMALS).
On-play +2 wood; then a mandatory before-hook on ANOTHER player taking >=5 wood from an accumulation space — they pay you 1 food (opponent-to-you transfer).
Returning-home-phase hook each round: if no wood anywhere on the board, gain 1 food (2 from round 5 on) — condition is a plain board-state read.
End-of-work-phase hook: needs a per-phase flag/counter of whether you PLACED (not merely moved — clarification) at least 1 good onto a farmyard space; then +2 food.
On-play optional tiered exchange 0/1/3 clay -> 1/2/3 grain (the 0-tier is a plain gain); the card itself is a field you can sow/harvest on.
Fires each time ANOTHER player uses the Hollow accumulation space; you get 1 clay (the 3/4-player extra good is the same mechanic).
Explicit 'each time after you use' Clay Pit / Hollow -> +1 wood (Clay Pit also +1 reed); mandatory gain on your own space use.

cards.new_category: HANDCOST: an effect cost paid by discarding an unplayed card from the player's hand
cards.note: Optional on each use of a building-resource accumulation space: discard 1 hand card (a cost no code covers) for +1 resource of the accumulating type.
'Each time after you use' a wood accumulation space, optionally pay 1 grain (ordinary resource cost, no code) for 1 wild boar — the boar must be accommodated.
On-play +1 sheep (E-ANIMALS); ongoing rule that each occupation card in front of you is a new 1-sheep holder (E-CAPNEW on cards).

On play: remove 3 supply stables from the game (piece cost) to fence a 2-space pasture at a flat discounted 2-wood fence cost (cost modification on the granted fence build); granted build is the play's own choice.

After using Farm Expansion / Major Improvement while the other is unoccupied, optionally pay 1 food to use the other space with the SAME person (worker moves there — ST-PLACELOG); errata caps the jump at once per turn (CAP-TURN prevents chaining).
One-time 1 stone on play; then a continuous rule (while wooden house with exactly 2 rooms) that treats all spaces except Meeting Place as unoccupied for you.
Fires after your Day Laborer use ('each time after'); optionally pay 1 food to also take Traveling Players or Lessons with the same placed person (two spaces, one worker).
Pure end-game scoring rule (sum major-improvement VPs, smallest doubled, threshold table); computed at scoring, no hook, no optimization over arrangements.
One-shot fires the first moment the standing condition (7 sheep on farm) becomes true: 3 bonus points + 2 food; the <7-sheep play restriction is a plain prerequisite.
On play +1 wood +1 reed; then each time you pay an occupation cost (play-a-card sub-action) you may pay with food sitting on the Traveling Players accumulation space — a payment-source extension.
'At any time' pay 1 animal (ordinary animal cost, no code) to place 1 food on each of the next 3/5/7 round spaces; clarification only bars splitting grouped acquisitions.
End-of-work-phase check whether YOUR workers used both Forest and Grove this phase (needs placement log); mandatory 5 food if so.
Fires on another player's plow (field placed adjacent to an existing field tile — farm adjacency); mandatory 1 food (grain in round 14).
1 grain on play, plus a mandatory end-of-work-phase check that Forest/Clay Pit/Reed Bank are all occupied (occupancy by any player, hence both actor tags) → 1 grain.
Fires at the end of each of your work-phase turns: if a 5+-good accumulation space existed and your placement wasn't on any of them (needs to know where you placed), you get 1 vegetable.
Obtain-keyed hook needing the event payload (exact wood amount + that the source was a wood accumulation space); clarification pins it before leave-wood-on-space decisions (Basket).
Optional, immediately before each harvest: discard planted grain from different fields (crop-on-field manipulation) for 1/2/3 wild boars (must be accommodated).
One-time optional on-play: pay 2 food for sheep+boar+cattle; the accommodate-all-three condition is a legality check on the same accommodation machinery E-ANIMALS already implies.
'Each time you renovate' → renovate sub-action hook (default before timing); mandatory 2 food + 1 animal of the matching type per stabled pasture (animals need accommodation).
On-play +1 grain; passive rule making each grain in supply a holder for 1 animal of any type (a new, supply-count-dependent animal slot).

One room piece stored on the card; once the standing condition (stone house, >=4 rooms) holds, at any time you may build that room free (a one-shot optional build-room grant, not a LATCH since it's deferrable, not auto-fired).
"Each time you use Grain Seeds" (default before) grants an optional plow-1-field sub-action.

cards.new_category: BEGGING-COST: an effect whose cost/penalty is taking begging markers
cards.note: Personal action space; per-use counter (1st-4th) scales the reward (6-9 different goods, which at 8-9 must include animals); each use also imposes 1 begging marker (no code covers begging as a cost).
On-play +3 food; auto hook on the harvest field phase paying +1 food per vegetable harvested from fields (needs the field-phase yield payload).
Optional Bake Bread sub-action on play and at the start of each feeding phase (before feeding resolves).

cards.new_category: SKIP-HARVEST: the player skips an entire upcoming harvest (no field/feed/breed phases, no feeding obligation)
cards.note: On play, flags the next harvest as skipped for this player — a whole-harvest exemption no existing code captures.
Optional conversion of exactly 1 vegetable -> 5 food, available during each harvest's feeding phase (once per feeding phase by nature).
After each harvest's field phase, optionally turn 1 grain into 3 food OR 1 bonus point — bonus points earned through the action need a running counter.
Immediately-before hook on three named spaces (clarified to precede other before-effects like Flail): optionally buy 1 grain for 1 food.
On-play +1 grain; "at the end of each harvest" mapped to after the breeding phase (no exact end-of-harvest seam exists): optionally buy 1 vegetable for 2 food.
On play, schedules 1 wood on each of the next 5 round spaces, collected at those rounds' starts.
On-play +1 wood +1 clay; after each renovation, auto-pays wood equal to people placed this round — needs a per-round placed-workers counter (newborns excluded per clarification).
On-play +1 wood +2 clay; auto +1 clay after each use of a Grain Seeds or Vegetable Seeds action space.
Always-on cost reduction: -1 clay on improvements/renovations, -2 clay on rooms.

cards.new_category: WORKPHASE-START: fires at the start of each work phase (effectively start of round, after preparation)
cards.note: Recurring automatic gain of 2 clay/stone at each work-phase start, gated on a standing state read (clay/stone house with exactly 2 rooms); S-SOR is the nearest seam.
Explicit 'after you use Day Laborer': mandatory +1 wood +1 clay (F-AUTO) plus an optional buy of 1 stone for 1 food (F-TRIG, food cost, conversion).

cards.new_category: SCORE-ALLPLAYERS: an end-game bonus rule that awards points to EVERY player meeting a condition, not just the card's owner
cards.note: On-play wood scaled by a plain rounds-left state read; scoring rule gives 3 bonus points to each (any) player with no negative points — not comparative, but scores the opponent too.
Optional bonus on using Grain Seeds (+1 grain +1 veg); if taken, each other player gains 1 grain from the general supply (opponent-gain side effect).
Fires on another player's renovate sub-action, automatic +1 reed; on-play +1 reed only in a 3-player game (a plain player-count condition).
Each use of the Cattle Market accumulation space yields 1 additional cattle (must be accommodated).
Optional exchange (2 wood -> 1 grain + 1 food + 1 bonus point) triggered by another player's renovate; bonus points earned through the action need a running counter.
On play places 1 cattle each on round spaces +5 and +8; collected at those rounds' start and must be accommodated.
Optional food-for-animal buy (sheep/boar/cattle at 1/2/2 food) each time ANOTHER player uses Fencing; animal must be accommodated.
Once-ever, at any time after reaching 5 rooms (a plain state-read condition, not a latch — usage is optional/deferrable): a free build-room sub-action; needs a used flag.
On using Farmland/Cultivation, optionally pay 1 food to plow 1 additional field (granted plow sub-action with a food cost).
Schedules FIELD TILES (pieces, not goods) on round spaces +4/+7/+10; at each such round's start the plow is optional and costs 1 food — a decision hook, not automatic collection.
On play +2 wood; after each room built, optionally build/play 1 improvement at 1 wood less (granted improvement action with a cost reduction scoped to that grant).

cards.new_category: BEGGING-MARKER: the card itself imposes/grants a begging marker as part of its effect
cards.note: On play: mandatory begging marker (no code covers gaining a begging marker). Then at the start of each harvest an optional grant to play 1 occupation (play-a-card primitive) with its occupation cost waived (cost modification).
Fires once, immediately before the round-14 (final) harvest — a fixed-time harvest-start hook, optional; sweeps all building resources off the board's accumulation spaces into supply.

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: the paid good is placed onto an action space to be collected by the next user of that space
cards.note: After each own use of Grain Utilization, optionally pay 1 food for 1 bonus point (needs a running bonus-point counter); the food goes onto the action space for the next visitor, a mechanic no code covers. Clarification blocks same-turn use when the card itself was played via that space.
Automatic +1 wood +1 food whenever you gain a new field tile — hooks the plow sub-action, but by wording covers ANY source of a new field tile (card-granted plows included).
Optional each returning-home phase: discard ALL your clay (>=1) for a choice of 3 food or 1 bonus point (bonus points need a running counter).
Once per harvest, an optional player-timed conversion of exactly 1 stone into 3 food.
Every renovation (sub-action) automatically schedules 1 food onto each of the next 6 round spaces.
On play stocks the card with 2 grain + 2 veg (a consumable on-card stack); at any time buy them at fixed food rates (2f/grain, 3f/veg) — a food-cost conversion.
In each breeding phase, per newborn actually accommodated, an optional granted sow of exactly 1 field (clarification: newborn must be accommodatable; no sowing onto Wood Field D075).
On play loads 6 wood onto the card; automatically pays out 1 wood from the stack immediately after each of YOUR card plays (including this one).
Before using any action SPACE offering Build Fences or Build Rooms: optional choice of +2 wood OR exchanging up to 2 wood at 1:1 for reed; clarified NOT to fire on card-granted actions (spaces only).
One-time on play: schedules 1 clay on each remaining round space 6-14, collected at those rounds' starts.
Always-on replacement cost formulas: clay renovation = exactly 1 clay + 1 reed; each clay room = 3 clay + 2 reed.
A scoring-time rule (computed, not fired): optionally pay 1 food per unused farmyard space to negate its -1 point penalty — a food cost payable at scoring.
On-play +1 grain; then a mandatory own-action space hook on Grain Seeds granting +1 grain each use (default T-BEFORE for 'each time you use').

Fires on taking from an accumulation space holding >=4 of one resource (per clarification); needs the event payload (which resource is accumulating / how many were on the space) -> ST-PROV; grants +1 of that resource +1 food, mandatory.
Explicitly 'immediately before' each of your own subsequent occupation plays (even before its cost) -> T-BEFORE on the play-a-card seam; mandatory 2 food.
Mandatory hook on using any action space during rounds 1-8 (fixed round band, not geometry); accumulates food ON the card, auto-flushing to supply at 3 -> ST-STACK.
On-play +2 clay; plus an 'at any time' clay->stone conversion at two rates (2:1, 3:2).

Fires on the build-stable sub-action when total stables reach 2/3/4 (ordinal readable from state; 'not retroactively' means only builds after play count); mandatory animal gain -> E-ANIMALS (accommodation needed).
Optional one-time purchase (1 grain + 2 food, food payable by conversion) available while living in clay/stone house; the card then permanently holds one person -> E-CAPNEW; needs an activated flag.
Pure always-on cost changes: wood rooms -2, 3rd/4th stables -1 wood, fences 13-15 free (fence count computable from state).
On-play optional build of exactly 1 stable for 1 wood (granted sub-action, optional); plus a passive capacity raise: one chosen unfenced stable holds up to 3 animals of a type.
Optional extra plow for 1 food when using Farmland/Cultivation with the round's first placed person (first-placement is computable from own workers on the board); food cost payable by conversion.
Optional Family Growth for 3 food immediately before each harvest (before the field phase), conditioned on having room in the house (normal growth, needs room).

cards.new_category: SPACE-SEED: place a good from the general supply onto another (named) action space, adding to what future users of that space will take
cards.note: Optional hook on Forest/Clay Pit: seed 1 stone from general supply onto the other space (no code covers adding goods to a live accumulation space) and, if done, immediately place another person; clarification allows firing twice in one turn.
'At any time' full Build Stables action without placing a person, with a replacement per-stable cost of 1 wood + 1 food (food payable by conversion).

On play, immediately (mandatory) builds the named Basketmaker's Workshop major improvement at a reduced fixed cost (1 stone + 1 reed) — an acquire-major action outside the normal space, with a cost override.

Fires immediately after taking a Maj/Min-Improvement action ('immediately after' -> T-AFTER); optional ('you can'); pay 1 food (E-FOODCOST) to repeat the whole action (E-GRANTACT). Clarification only excludes a card combo, no mechanic.

Triggers on people (any player's) returning home from both Grain Seeds AND Vegetable Seeds (S-ROUNDEND, both actors). 2 food is mandatory (F-AUTO); the play-occupation grant is optional (F-TRIG) and waives the occupation cost (E-COSTMOD); playing a card is a granted sub-action (E-GRANTSUB).

On play +1 wood. Each harvest's field phase (once per occurrence, no CAP needed) you may exchange 1 wood + 1 food (E-FOODCOST) for 1 bonus point — action-earned bonus points accumulate over the game (ST-COUNTER).

A real fired decision at start of scoring (not a passive scoring rule): optionally distribute food across empty fields — an optimization over the 1/4/7-food tiers (E-SCOREOPT), costing food (E-FOODCOST). Errata makes the food count as planted on fields (E-CROPMANIP — it changes field contents, affecting planted-field reads).

Pure end-game bonus rule (4 pts if rooms > people), computed at scoring — no hook. Must remember whether it was played in round 11 or earlier (ST-STORE flag); the round-11 condition itself is a plain computable check.
'Once per round, you can' = ATWILL + CAP-ROUND. Exchange 2 grain for 1 bonus point (action-earned, ST-COUNTER) + food scaled by unfenced-stable count (a plain state read).

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: place goods from your own supply onto an action space's accumulation pile (where any player may later collect them)
cards.note: 'Each time after you use' a wood accumulation space (T-AFTER, S-SPACE), optional exchange of 4 wood for goods — but the 4 wood is DEPOSITED on the board space, a mechanic no code covers.
Start-of-each-round optional pick of exactly one exchange (choose-one but declinable -> F-TRIG). Several exchanges yield animals (cattle/sheep) that must be accommodated (E-ANIMALS).
'Once each harvest, you can buy' — deferrable at-will purchase within each harvest (ATWILL + CAP-HARVEST); pays 2 food (E-FOODCOST) for goods.
Fires before/at the start of each round (S-SOR), mandatory ('you get'), +1 food gated on a plain state read (food >= round number).

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: place goods from your own supply onto an action space's accumulation pile (where any player may later collect them)
cards.note: Same shape as Tree Guard C102: after using a clay/stone accumulation space, optionally deposit 1 stone / 2 clay ON that space to gain 2 grain / 1 veg respectively (the choice matches the space used).

Two parts: mandatory hook on the build-major-improvement sub-action ('each time you build' -> S-SUB) placing 1 general-supply reed on this card (a stack, ST-STACK); plus 'at any time' (ATWILL) cash a stored reed to supply (E-GOODS) or trade it for a Sow sub-action (E-GRANTSUB, optional).
On play +1 wood. Then each later own occupation play grants wood equal to the food paid as its occupation cost — needs the event's payment payload (ST-PROV).

'Each time you use' an action space -> T-BEFORE, S-SPACE; mandatory +1 wood. Condition depends on orthogonal adjacency between action-space cards and on which spaces your own people occupy (L-GEOMBOARD; occupancy is standard board state).
On play seeds an 8-clay pile on the card (ST-STACK); thereafter each grain obtained (any source -> S-OBTAIN) automatically pays 1 clay from the pile until empty.

Optional buy of exactly 2 stone during each harvest's breeding phase; price is fixed by harvest index (computable from round). Timing within the phase is unordered w.r.t. breeding — T-BEFORE chosen as it doesn't interact with the breed itself.

Optional pre-work-phase placement of one of your (normal) people, restricted to accumulation spaces of a building resource not in your supply. It's an out-of-turn EARLY placement, not an extra person — nearest code is E-EXTRAPLACE.
On play, pay food = complete rounds remaining (mandatory; unaffordable => unplayable) to take a Family Growth EVEN WITHOUT ROOM sub-action, without placing a person as part of the card play.
Always-on replacement cost formula for wood rooms; the wood component varies by current round number (fixed schedule, plain state read).
Continuous legality extension: may use Urgent Wish for Children while occupied, but only if exactly one (another player's first-placed) person is there — computable from the space's occupancy.

cards.new_category: SPACE-SEED: place goods from the general supply onto another (current, non-future) accumulation/action space
cards.note: On using Grove/Hollow, optionally seed 2 reed from general supply onto the other space (no code covers seeding a current board space; E-SCHED is future rounds only), and if done, immediately place another person (chainable per clarification).

On using Grain Seeds while any Lessons is occupied, optionally play an occupation (play-a-card sub-action grant) for a flat 1-food cost; the newly played occupation's Grain-Seeds trigger fires immediately per clarification.
Fires on the renovate-to-stone sub-action: optionally place up to 1 wood per room onto the card/rooms (ST-STORE counts placed wood); each stored wood is a bonus point at scoring.

End-game bonus per stone-wood pair in supply; the 'cannot score additional points for these resources' clause is a cross-card resource-scoring exclusion — nearest code E-SCOREGRP (may need resource-assignment bookkeeping if other resource-scoring cards coexist).
End-game bonus per farmyard space (incl. rooms) holding >=1 cattle — rewards SPREADING cattle across holders, a genuine arrangement optimization over animal placement.

On play, tiered immediate wood conditioned on rounds remaining (plain state read); plus a comparative end-game rule — each player holding a pasture of the (game-wide) highest capacity gets 3 bonus points.

Automatic 1 wood + 1 food whenever ANY player plays OR builds a cooking improvement (minor played -> S-PLAY; major built -> the Major/Minor action seam); needs the event payload (which improvement) to test 'cooking' -> ST-PROV.
Riding on Day Laborer: mandatory choice of 1 sheep or 1 grain (no decline), optionally replaced by buying 1 boar for 1 food or 1 cattle for 2 food; animals must be accommodated.
On-play gain 1 reed + 1 food; standing at-will conversion 1 reed -> 2 food (uncapped).
On-play +1 grain; each time you get a Minor Improvement action (incl. granted ones), you may take a Bake Bread sub-action instead of it.
Fires on ANY player using Sheep Market ('each time... uses' -> before); mandatory 1 grain to owner.
Optional on-play buy 2 stone for 1 food; then once per round buy 1 stone for 2 food starting NEXT round (needs the play-round stored to gate the start).
'Each time after any player uses' Grove/Forest while the other is occupied ('unoccupied' space condition is a plain occupancy state read) -> mandatory 1 reed.
On play stocks a resource-pair pile on each of your improvements (goods stored on cards = ST-STACK); each time another player renovates you MAY reclaim one pair to your supply.

Each own accumulation-space use auto-adds 1 clay (general supply) to the card's pile; mandatory 4-clay-on-card -> 1 boar HELD BY the card (card is a new animal holder; boars can breed, newborn housed elsewhere).

Fires when another player renovates with target stone (needs the renovation's target = event payload; clarification: wood->stone also fires); if you live in clay house, optionally pay 2 food to build a clay room at no additional cost (cost replaced).

On your turn, pay 1 grain to use the action space the right-hand player just used (occupied space; needs to know their last placement; not usable on an emptied accumulation space). Text doesn't say 'without placing a person', so assumed to consume your normal placement. E-GOODS only insofar as the used space grants; core is the grant + occupancy override.
'Each time after another player uses' Grain Utilization -> you get a Sow sub-action (granted, hence optional -> F-TRIG).
When another player uses Traveling Players, optionally pay THEM 1 food (opp-transfer) to immediately play an occupation with its occupation cost waived (play-a-card grant + cost mod).

Each own use of a twinned accumulation space when both same-good spaces hold exactly equal counts (checked before taking) -> optional buy 1 bonus point for 1 food; bought bonus points accumulate over the game.

On-play +1 grain plus a ONE-SHOT deferred payout at the start of THIS round's returning-home: food = number of occupied round-1..14 action-space cards (plain state read; fires once only, this round — not a recurring hook).

cards.new_category: BOARD-SEED: on play, add a good/animal from the general supply onto an existing accumulation space's pile (benefiting whoever later takes it)
cards.note: Adds 1 cattle to the Cattle Market pile (board modification, not the player's animals — so no E-ANIMALS), then gains 1 grain + 1 food per cattle now on that space (state read).
Fires before each round starts (before the refill), auto-grants 1 food on a plain comparative state read of both players' building-resource counts.
Fires before every own worker placement (any space); condition is a plain board read (>=8 wood on accumulation spaces); mandatory 1 food.
Start-of-round auto hook; reads two NAMED spaces' food counts (explicitly not L-GEOMBOARD) and pays the difference from supply. Tagged T-AFTER as it reads post-refill amounts at round start.
Before-use hook on a space identified by reveal ORDER (most recently revealed round card) — board-geometry dependent; auto 1 grain.
One-shot on play: vegetables scaled by a plain state read (count of unplanted field tiles); trivially implementable.

The card is itself an action space usable by all (L-CARDSPACE, passive); its resolution gives the user wood, and an opponent's use additionally auto-gives the owner 1 wood FROM THE GENERAL SUPPLY (so not E-OPPTRANSFER).

Fires on ANY player's accumulation-space take; needs the event payload (how many goods were taken: 5/6/7/8+ maps to wood/clay/reed/stone) — ST-PROV; T-AFTER since it keys on the amount actually taken.
'Each time any player uses [space]' → before-use hook on Pig Market, both actors; grants a sheep from supply which must be accommodated (E-ANIMALS).
One-shot on play: mandatory food cost scaled by remaining harvests (a fixed-schedule state read, per clarification excluding an in-progress harvest), yielding 1 cattle + 1 boar to accommodate.

Optional substitute for Day Laborer's 2 food (3 different animals instead); electing it creates a standing 1-food obligation at each remaining harvest feeding phase (S-HFEED + E-FOODCOST), stacking per use → ST-COUNTER.

Per the project clarification the play prerequisite is hardcoded at 7 occupations, so the 6-occupation condition is always satisfied on play — the card is simply a passive room-for-one-person holder (E-CAPNEW), no latch needed.
Always-on rule: each occupation card (not already animal-holding) becomes a 1-sheep holder — new slots, no animals provided.

On-play +1 grain; then a standing cost conversion on fence/stable/room builds and renovations (up to 2 building resources each replaced by 1 grain, per clarification: per build action) — a payment-substitution cost modifier.
Triggered by the build-fence sub-action (at least 1 fence built); grants an optional ('you can also') wood-free build-stable sub-action alongside it.

From round 5 (plain state read), after each of your person actions you may take Family Growth with that person (optional grant -> F-TRIG); needs room in house; each use accrues 2 negative points (action-earned scoring, counted over the game).
Once per work phase, after completing a person action, optionally pay 1 sheep + 2 food (food cost) to return another placed person home for reuse; needs to know which persons are placed where.

Triggers on taking a Build Rooms action, but only when done with your SECOND placed person; mandatorily returns your first placed person home (unless on Meeting Place) — needs placement order + which space each worker sits on.
On play, immediately build a major improvement (granted action, optional), with a cost substitution: up to 1 resource of each type replaceable by 1 food each (cost modifier paid partly in food).

Play restriction (round <=4) is a plain condition. After the final harvest (a fired pre-scoring event, not a passive rule), you optionally pay 1 clay per person for 1 bonus point each — action-earned bonus points, so HOOK+E-SCORE is legitimate.
Pure end-game scoring rule: 1 bonus point per scoring category maxed at 4 points (incl. 4 fenced stables). Computed at scoring; no hook.
At the start of each returning-home phase, optionally return a BUILT stable (component return, not a supply piece) for 1 wood + 1 grain + 1 food plus a granted Minor Improvement action.
On using Fishing or Reed Bank, optionally pay 1 food to place an ADDITIONAL person on this card (the card is the placement destination), gaining a player-chosen (3 stone) or (1 grain + 1 vegetable).
Automatic goods rider on using any clay accumulation space (+1 food) or stone accumulation space (+1 grain); 'each time you use' -> before-timing default.
'At any time' pay 1 grain (ordinary cost) to place 4 food on the round space two rounds ahead, collected at that round's start — deferrable at-will scheduling.

+1 wood on play; then an explicit AFTER hook ('each time after you use') on any action space that includes the Sow action, granting 2 food automatically. Keyed on the space offering Sow, not on actually sowing.

cards.new_category: ACCUM-BOOST: conditionally adds extra goods onto ACCUMULATION action spaces (not future round spaces), scaled by another accumulation space's current stock
cards.note: Reads the food count on Fishing (1/2/3+) and adds 2 extra food for you onto Reed Bank / Clay Pit / Forest (per errata) — accumulation-space modification each round; no existing code covers goods placed on accumulation spaces.
On using the Major Improvement space: 1 grain automatic (before-default), plus an explicitly 'afterward' OPTIONAL granted Sow sub-action (granted sub-actions are optional -> F-TRIG).

During the harvest field phase, per grain harvested you may buy 1 vegetable for 3 food (2 food if it was the LAST grain on that field) — a food-priced conversion needing the harvest event's provenance (which field, whether emptied).

Personal '1 Wood' accumulation space (L-CARDSPACE, wood piles on card = ST-STACK, refills at start of each round = S-SOR); a second mandatory hook on the round-card reveal (S-REVEAL, F-AUTO) discards all accumulated wood when a Quarry is revealed (E-TAKEBACK).
On play +2 wood; ongoing passive cost modifier on every improvement (up to 2 wood less) where the substitution is paid with 1 food (E-FOODCOST).
On play +2 clay; 'at any time, once per round' buy of 2 clay for 2 food = ATWILL + CAP-ROUND conversion paid in food.

Fires on each own build-room sub-action ('for each new room you build' = automatic reward after the build); payout keyed to the room's material (wood room->2 clay, clay room->2 stone), computable from state.

At-will conversion: place one good from supply onto the card to gain 1 stone; goods accumulate on the card and each must be a DIFFERENT good type (the on-card pile is the memory of which types were used).
'Each time you use a wood or clay accumulation space' = own-action space hook (T-BEFORE default), optional buy of exactly 1 building resource at fixed food prices.
On play stacks a fixed 7-good pile on the card (ST-STACK); each field tile harvested in the field phase optionally yields the top good of the pile.
Personal action space providing three other spaces' full actions (Day Laborer + Building Rooms + Major Improvement), gated on a plain state comparison (every opponent has more rooms).
Fires after ANOTHER player's build-room sub-action; optional: pay them 1 food (opponent transfer, food cost) to build exactly 1 room yourself at normal cost (granted build-room sub-action).

Per-harvest optional grant of a full 'Build Stables' or wood-limited 'Build Rooms' action at usual costs, conditioned on holding >=5 wood; 'discard down to 5 wood' is a wood-discard cost. Exact point within the harvest is unstated — tagged start-of-harvest (S-HSTART).

End-of-work-phase hook granting an optional full 'Build Rooms' action without placing a person, conditional on NOT having used Meeting Place that phase (needs the record of which spaces your workers used).
On play +1 stone; passive legality extension — bottom-row major improvements become buildable via a 'Minor Improvement' action (fixed supply-board rows, not variable geometry).
Optional per-feeding-phase conversion: 1 wood + 1 grain -> 2 food + 1 bonus point (a point earned through an action, so HOOK+E-SCORE is legitimate; accumulated bonus points need a running counter).

cards.new_category: SKIPTURN: the player is forced to skip/defer their next worker placement within the current round (turn-order manipulation without touching a placed worker)
cards.note: Fires whenever ANY player (including you) uses Fishing: mandatory +1 bonus point (counter) AND you must skip your next placement that round (a pending-skip flag; no existing code covers forced placement-skipping).
Immediate wood scaled by a plain rounds-left read; end-game comparative bonus (most vegetables in fields, checked after final harvest) is a scoring rule, no hook.
Immediate wood by rounds-left; comparative end-game bonus for most fenced stables.
After using a Lessons space, optional purchase of up to 2 different goods at food rates (includes animals); clarification: not triggered by playing this card itself.
On using a 1-animal accumulation space, may leave the animal and take one from supply instead plus 3 food + 1 grain — replaces the take, so fires before the space's collection.
Fires on either player's Meeting Place use (explicitly 'before taking the actions'); both get supply food on opponent use, you alone on own use — supply food, not a transfer.
Keyed on the AMOUNT actually taken from an accumulation space (>=4 resources/animals) — needs the take's payload; bonus food alongside the take.
End-of-work-phase check: your worker on Clay Pit XOR Reed Bank (other unoccupied) yields 1 veg — needs worker-location knowledge at phase end.
'Each time you use' an accumulation space providing >=3 same-type non-wood goods (incl. food) -> +1 wood; condition reads the space's provided count (snapshot before zeroing).
'After you use' Fishing or its three orthogonally adjacent spaces -> +1 reed; adjacency between action spaces is board geometry.
One-time reed scaled by a plain count of owned major improvements — trivial state read.
Each Day Laborer use schedules food/food/boar onto the next 3 round spaces; the boar must eventually be accommodated.
Always-on: each orthogonally-adjacent room pair's border becomes a new 2-sheep holder — new slots + farm adjacency; negated by Milking Place per clarification.
On another player's Quarry use, optional choice: 1 food OR a free build-stable sub-action (granted sub-actions optional -> F-TRIG).

Fires on a Family Growth taken with your SECOND placement of the round: returns your first-placed person home (reusable this phase) — needs per-round placement order + which space; worded mandatorily.
'Immediately after' using Traveling Players (T-AFTER), optionally place an additional person (E-EXTRAPLACE) on any space even if occupied (L-OCCUPY).

Fires automatically at the start of each harvest (harvest index is fixed by round number, no extra state); grants a bonus point if grain-field count meets the harvest's threshold — bonus points earned through a fired event, so HOOK + E-SCORE.
Always-on renovation discount (E-COSTMOD) plus an end-game scoring rule that reads OTHER players' house material (E-SCORE + E-SCORECMP); scoring is computed, not hooked.
'Each harvest, you can' = player-chosen use once per harvest: convert 1 wood into 1 food + 1 grain.
On play stocks 3 grain + 3 food onto the card (ST-STACK); each use of Resource Market automatically draws 1 grain + 1 food from that pile ('each time you use' default T-BEFORE).

One-shot fires the first moment ANOTHER player's person-count reaches 5 (standing count condition → LATCH/ST-THRESHOLD, not retroactive), giving 8 food; plus an end-game bonus (3 pts if only you have 5 people — depends on both players, E-SCORECMP).

cards.new_category: OPP-VETO: another player may preempt/cancel your declared at-will effect by paying you a price (buying the reed for 2 food)
cards.note: At-any-time 1 reed → 3 food conversion; an opponent can intercept it by paying you 2 food for the reed (goods flow between players = E-OPPTRANSFER; the interrupt/veto itself has no code).
Fires when another player's FIRST-placed person of the round takes Family Growth (needs per-round placement-order tracking: ST-COUNTER/ST-PLACELOG); you automatically get 1 grain.
After ANY player's renovation resolves (incl. whether a minor/major improvement followed it — event payload, ST-PROV), you may buy 1 veg at a price scaled by that outcome (food cost → E-FOODCOST).
On play +2 stone; then automatically +1 stone whenever another player renovates TO STONE or builds a stone room (needs the renovation target / room material from the event — ST-PROV).
After using an animal accumulation space, if the house pet slot is empty ('if afterward' → T-AFTER), you also get 1 sheep (must be accommodated → E-ANIMALS).

On using an animal accumulation space, +1 boar if one of YOUR workers occupies the board space directly above/below it — action-board adjacency (L-GEOMBOARD) plus knowing where your workers sit (ST-PLACELOG).
Triggers on the build-stable sub-action once ≥2 stables are built in the same turn (per-turn running count → ST-COUNTER); automatically +1 cattle.
On play +1 wood; after each non-harvest round-end you MAY (F-TRIG) breed one animal type — breeding outside the harvest breeding phase (E-BREEDMOD) yielding newborns (E-ANIMALS).

Hook fires each time boar/cattle is converted to food (nearest seam: obtaining food from that conversion); food accumulates on the card (ST-STACK); while food==rooms the card is a continuous extra room holding 1 person (PASSIVE + E-CAPNEW).
At-any-time wood placement onto the card (irretrievable pile = ST-STACK); the card is a new animal holder with capacity 2x its wood (PASSIVE capacity rule + E-CAPNEW).
Fires at end of work phase of rounds 7 and 9 (fixed rounds, no geometry); optional granted renovation without placing a person, 1 building resource cheaper.
Recurring start-of-round option gated on a standing state read (stone house — NOT a latch, it recurs); optional fence build at a replacement price (2 wood->3 fences / 3 wood->4 fences).
Fires after playing your 2nd/3rd/5th/7th occupation (occupation count is readable from state, incl. this card itself); optional free stable build (grant + no-cost).
Fires on gaining 2+ newborns in breeding; optional pay-1-food to plow 1 field. Clarification (must accommodate newborns) is the normal breeding rule, no extra mechanic.

cards.new_category: S-USECARD: a trigger keyed on the player USING a specific improvement card's conversion ability (here the Joinery during harvest), not on a space/phase
cards.note: Two parts: passive legality extension (Joinery buildable via a Minor Improvement action = L-EXT); plus a hook on using the Joinery/upgrade during harvest granting pay-1-food-plow-1-field — nearest seam is the harvest feeding window (S-HFEED) but the true trigger is card-usage, hence the new_category.

Once-per-game (used flag = ST-STORE) legality extension: use a Wish for Children space even without room (E-GROWTH 'even without room'); condition is farm geometry (2-room house surrounded by 4 field tiles = L-GEOMFARM).

cards.new_category: S-TURNSTART: fires on/at a player's own Nth turn of a round (here: your first turn each round) — no existing seam matches a turn-index trigger
cards.note: Optional on your first turn each round if no unused farmyard spaces (state read): place a person FROM YOUR SUPPLY — an extra worker beyond your family (E-PEOPLE) placed additionally (E-EXTRAPLACE); text does not say the person permanently joins, engine should treat per errata/ruling.
On play: an immediate Renovation (granted sub-action, usual cost) followed by an optional full Build Fences action (granted action); both at normal cost, so no cost mechanic.

On play optionally build a baking improvement at cost (granted build action); recurring hook when ANOTHER player uses Grain Seeds (A-OPP, 'each time uses' defaults T-BEFORE) granting an optional Bake Bread sub-action.
No effect beyond counting as a played occupation; its mechanic is the special play window — playable at the start of round 1's work phase without placing a person (E-NOPLACE).

On play, if 3rd occupation (state read): grants playing another occupation (at a modified 1-food play cost = E-COSTMOD + E-FOODCOST) and/or a minor improvement at its printed cost (play-a-card grants tagged E-GRANTSUB, the play-card primitive).
On play, if 1st occupation: bonus points fixed at play time (1 per improvement then owned) — the earned amount must be snapshotted (ST-STORE) since later improvements don't count.
Fires at the end of each harvest (no exact seam; tagged as after the breeding phase, the harvest's last step); plain stone-house state read; accumulates bonus points during play (ST-COUNTER).
Fires at the start of each work phase (closest seam: start of round, before work); plain supply-count check; accumulates bonus points over the game.

cards.new_category: PLAY-RESTRICT: permanently prohibits the player from taking a class of future actions (here: may not play any more occupations)
cards.note: One-time bonus points computed from stages remaining at play time; the occupation ban is an always-on legality restriction no code captures.
Optional start-of-round purchase; food cost scales with people count (may need conversion), buys 1 good of choice from supply.

On play stacks clay/wood/grain on the card (consumed top-down); obtain-keyed optional trigger needs the obtained good's identity (ST-PROV) matched against the stack top; grants a boar (must be accommodated).
Play-timing condition (round <=4) is a plain state read; schedules 3 vegetables onto the round-11 space.
On-play goods plus a before-use hook keyed on the MOST RECENTLY REVEALED action space (board reveal-order dependency); mandatory gain with a resource-of-choice pick.
One-time optional bulk conversion at play (wood/clay->2 food, reed/stone->3 food), capped by people count (plain state read).
Automatic food in each harvest field phase, tiered on field count (plain state read).
After each build-fences sub-action, schedules 1 food per remaining round space capped by fences JUST built (needs the event's fence-count payload).

Once-each-harvest optional reed->2 food conversion; plus an always-on rule letting the Basketmaker's Workshop be built via a Minor Improvement action (legality extension) at a replacement cost of 1 reed + 1 stone (cost mod).
Two hooks: optional wood placement onto the card at each harvest start (F-TRIG), and automatic food per wood-on-card in each feeding phase (F-AUTO); the irretrievable wood pile lives on the card.
Automatic 1 food each round start and 1 wood each harvest start, gated on the plain state read 'no minor improvements played'.
Optional per-grain-field replacement during the field phase: leave the crop on the field (changes the field-harvest rule) and take 1 grain from supply instead.
Fires each time you take a Family Growth sub-action ('each time you take/use' = before per default); mandatory +1 vegetable.
Hook on the build-stable sub-action; reward keyed to ordinal of stables built AFTER play, so needs a snapshot/counter excluding pre-existing stables (ST-STORE).
Explicit 'each time after you use' Grain/Vegetable Seeds; grants an optional Bake Bread or Sow sub-action respectively.
On-play +1 food; then each time AFTER using an animal accumulation space, mandatory wood scaled by which person (1st-5th) was placed — derivable from workers placed count, no stored state.
Start of each harvest (before field phase), mandatory 2 wood if a plain state condition (>=1 grain field) holds.
Obtain-good hook restricted by provenance (food specifically from an action space) — needs the event's source payload; mandatory +1 wood.
Round<=4 play restriction is a computable prerequisite; on play schedules 4 wood + 4 clay on the round-9 space, collected at that round's start.
On play schedules alternating 1 wood / 1 clay on the next 6 round spaces, collected at those rounds' starts.
'Each time you use' Grain/Vegetable Seeds = before; mandatory 2 or 3 clay depending on which space.
Fires immediately after paying an improvement's cost (play minor or build major = play/build-improvement sub-action); mandatory but must choose 1 wood OR 1 clay.

On play stacks 6 resources in fixed order on the card; the top item(s) are optionally usable as payment when building a room, playing/building an improvement, or renovating — an on-card ordered stack acting as an extra payment source.
On-play +2 wood +2 stone; end-game scoring rule of -1 per wood/stone in supply; plus an always-on rule change forbidding discarding wood/stone.
On-play 1 building resource of choice; then a one-shot deferred optional extra placement once all people have been placed this round (mapped to the end-of-work-phase seam; fires this round only).
Not a LATCH — a recurring start-of-round effect whose stone-house condition is re-read each round; mandatory choice of 2 wood / 2 clay / 1 reed / 1 stone.

On play, if a computable scoring-state condition holds (max 4 pts in 3 categories), optionally grants a free build-room sub-action; condition is a state read (needs the scoring computation), not a mechanic.
After building a major improvement (a build-improvement sub-action, whichever route), optionally pay 1 food (food cost) for a granted plow sub-action.
Always-on rule: while one of your people is on Day Laborer (needs worker-location knowledge), occupied non-accumulating round-1-9 spaces are usable; the round-1-9 band is fixed, not board geometry.
Each use of a Wish for Children space (default before-timing) optionally grants an additional play-improvement action at its cost minus 1 resource of choice (cost modifier).

Explicit 'immediately after' placing your LAST person of the round on Traveling Players (last-person = plain workers-remaining read): optional grant of a play-occupation action at a replaced cost of 1 food.

Per-harvest optional conversion (1 grain + 1 veg -> 6 food) = ATWILL capped per harvest; at scoring an optional exchange of up to 3 pairs for bonus points, a real optimization since exchanged crops leave normal crop scoring (per clarification).
Fires automatically after placing 2/3+ newborns in each breeding phase, awarding bonus points through play (HOOK + E-SCORE per ruling); only accommodated newborns count.

Each harvest optionally diverts 1 harvested crop (field phase) or 1 accommodatable newborn (breed phase) onto the card as an irretrievable pile (ST-STACK); once per game an at-will exchange of 2-5 distinct stored goods for 3-9 bonus points.
Immediate wood scaled by rounds remaining (plain state read), plus a comparative most-vegetables scoring rule (both players compared; counts field + supply veg).
Immediate wood scaled by rounds remaining plus a granted (optional) full Build Fences action, and a comparative most-pastures scoring rule.
Two automatic action-space riders: using Reed Bank also gives 1 grain, using Grain Seeds also gives 1 reed ('each time you use' = before-timing, mandatory gain).
On play (only round <=11, a play restriction), choose an animal type and gain animals equal to your current count of it — animals must be accommodated.
On play, schedules (round chosen minus current round) food onto a chosen future round space, collected at that round's start; round-space selection is fixed numbering, not board geometry.
One-round window (needs a stored active-round flag): each use of a building-resource accumulation space automatically pays 1 food per resource taken — needs the taken-amount payload (ST-PROV).

Two space hooks: Major Improvement space -> +1 veg (F-AUTO goods); Vegetable Seeds space -> a granted Major/Minor Improvement action (granted action = optional, F-TRIG). 'Each time you use' = T-BEFORE default.
Optional exchanges (wood->grain, grain->stone, chainable to wood->stone) during each feeding phase; up-to-2 limit is per firing, no CAP needed (per-harvest by nature).
End of each work phase (round>=3), conditional on all clay accumulation spaces unoccupied (plain board read) -> +1 stone +1 food, mandatory.
Fires when ANY player plays/builds a resource->food converter card; needs the played card's identity to determine the 'corresponding' building resource (ST-PROV).
On play (round<=7 is a computable play restriction), choose clay or reed and double your current supply of it — immediate goods with a one-time choice.
Once ever, immediately after you play/build an improvement, optionally gain its printed cost; needs which improvement was built (ST-PROV) + a used flag (ST-STORE).
Start of each harvest: fenced-stable count 1/2/3+ -> sheep/boar/cattle (animal must be accommodated); mandatory.

On play seeds up to 4 of your stable pieces onto named spaces; when an opponent uses such a space you build that stable free (granted build = optional); needs per-space stable-remaining flags (ST-STORE).
Start of the LAST harvest only (fires once by nature): optionally take up to 2 unbuilt fences per opponent (pieces from another player) and build them free — farm may exceed the 15-fence supply cap.
Always-on: may use stone+other-resource spaces even if occupied (L-OCCUPY) and stone rooms cost 2 stone less (E-COSTMOD).
Once per game (used flag), if you hold all animal types, a Wish for Children space works even without room — legality extension on family growth ('even without room').
Start of each round, optional: pay 1 food (placed ON the Traveling Players accumulation space, not supply) to play a minor improvement at its cost (play-a-card granted sub-action).

'Each harvest you can' = at-will once per harvest: exchange 1 stone for 1 food + 1 bonus point; bonus points accumulate (ST-COUNTER); score earned through an action, so E-SCORE alongside ATWILL is fine.

'Once you live in a stone house' gates ONGOING effects (a standing state read, not a one-shot LATCH): +2 food each time ANY player renovates (renovate sub-action hook), plus end-game 1 bp per wood/clay house — reads both players' houses (E-SCORECMP).

cards.new_category: L-FORBID: a rule that BLOCKS/forbids an action (here family growth) until a round or an opponent-state condition is met
cards.note: On-play conditional (round<=4, plain state read) goods + 2 boar (E-ANIMALS); plus an ongoing passive PROHIBITION on family growth until round 11 unless all other players have grown - a legality restriction no code covers.
Fires on an OPPONENT playing/building any improvement; needs the played card's printed cost (has clay?) -> ST-PROV; mandatory gain of 1 food + 1 clay.
One-shot on play: food = count of other occupations in play (capped 7), gated on being your first occupation - all plain state reads, immediate goods only.
Fires automatically at the end of each work phase; food = plain count of stone accumulation spaces holding stone (board state read).

cards.new_category: FEED-REDUCE: passively reduces the per-person food requirement in the feeding phase
cards.note: Always-on feeding-rule change (1 less food per person - no code for feeding-cost modification) plus a scoring-rule override (people worth 2 instead of 3, a negative E-SCORE modification).
Fires when ANOTHER player uses Fishing (space-use default T-BEFORE); mandatory: opponent gets +1 food (E-OPPTRANSFER) and owner gets 1 veg.

The card IS a personal action space granting 3 grain when used (modeled as the space's own action); separately a passive legality extension letting Stone Oven (a major) be built via a Minor Improvement action.

Optional start-of-round choice: pay 1 food onto the card (a food pile ON the card, later discardable from it -> ST-STACK) or discard 1 from it, gaining 1 building resource of a type you lack (state read); the food payment is E-FOODCOST. SOR seam has no natural before/after - T-BEFORE tagged as before the round's work.
Fires after each subsequent occupation YOU play; mandatory gain but with a choice of building resource -> F-MANDCHOICE.
Fires after each plow sub-action ('that you just plowed'); sheep count = number of tiles plowed in that event -> needs the event payload (ST-PROV).
1 boar on play, plus 1 boar each time you build a MAJOR improvement (improvement-build tagged as S-PLAY, consistent with plays/builds-an-improvement hooks; typically via the Major/Minor action).

cards.new_category: BOARD-MOVE: relocates accumulated goods between two action spaces on the main board
cards.note: Optional on using Traveling Players or Fishing (space-use, T-BEFORE): move 1 food from the used space to the other (manipulating board accumulation piles - no existing code) to gain 1 cattle.

On play: EVERY player (incl. opponent - an out-of-turn opponent CHOICE of 2 sheep or 2 food, goods flowing to the other player -> E-OPPTRANSFER) plus 1 cattle for the owner; sheep/cattle need accommodation.

Automatic start-of-round animal income keyed on a plain state read: 1 sheep/boar/cattle per unoccupied wood/clay/stone room respectively (room material -> animal type mapping); animals need accommodation.
